title: Calathea lutea
uses: Calathea lutea is known to have several medicinal uses, primarily in traditional medicine in certain parts of the world. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help in treating various health issues. It is also used to treat digestive problems, including diarrhea and nausea. Additionally, Calathea lutea has been used to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory infections. More research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Calathea lutea for medicinal purposes.
taxonomy: Calathea lutea is classified as a member of the family Marantaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the prayer plants. This family is native to the tropical regions of the Americas and Africa. Calathea lutea is further classified as a species within the genus Calathea, which comprises various species of flowering plants known for their attractive foliage and vibrant colors. The genus Calathea is a member of the order Zingiberales, which is a diverse group of flowering plants that includes ginger and banana plants. Within the order Zingiberales, the family Marantaceae is a distinct group characterized by its unique leaf and flower morphology.

side_effects: Calathea lutea is a plant that is not typically known for causing side effects in humans, but its sap can cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals. intro: Calathea lutea, also known as the yellow calathea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing pain and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Calathea lutea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
